sections.title: Getting Around Orlando
sections.summary: Orlando is built for driving, not transit. A rental car or rideshare is how most solo visitors get around — there's no subway, and public buses are limited at night.
bullets: Rental car: the most freedom for park-hopping and day trips ($35–60/day).
Uber & Lyft: reliable and the safest option after dark.
I-Ride Trolley: cheap and useful along International Drive until about 10:30pm.
Resort shuttles: free buses, monorail, and boats connect on-site Disney hotels.
